instructions.text: Mix the flour, low-fat quark, baking powder, salt, olive oil, wheat bran, and water until a smooth dough forms.
Divide the dough into about 6 equal portions.
Flatten each portion to about 0.5 cm thick.
Cook the flatbreads in a hot frying pan until golden brown on both sides.
Finely chop the spring onions.
Blend the spring onions, chopped or strained tomatoes, curry spices, and peanut butter into a smooth sauce.
Pour the sauce into a pan and add the chickpeas and large white beans.
Stir in the heavy cream and bring everything to a boil.
Serve hot with flatbread.
Take 1 empty glass from the cupboard
Fill it with Sparkly water
Top it up with Tap water
Enjoy a fresh Schwäbische Schorle: Erfrischend, billig, ... ahhh bekömmlich!
The day before, prepare the poolish by mixing the water, flour, dry yeast, and honey. Cover and let it rest overnight at room temperature.
Mix the finished poolish with the remaining water.
Add the flour and salt and mix until no dry flour remains.
Let the dough rest for 15 minutes.
Transfer the dough to a work surface and knead it into a smooth ball.
Cover the dough and let it rest for 30 to 60 minutes.
Divide the dough into 10 equal portions and shape each into a tight dough ball.
Let the dough balls proof for 2 hours before stretching and baking.
Make a shortcrust pastry by mixing the flour, sugar, salt, vanilla sugar, baking powder, eggs, and butter. Divide the dough in half, wrap each half in plastic wrap, and chill for 30 minutes.
Roll out the first half of the dough on baking paper and transfer it to a deep rectangular baking tray.
Mix the apple sauce with the ground cinnamon and raisins, then spread the mixture evenly over the dough.
Roll out the second half of the dough, place it over the apple filling, and prick it several times with a fork.
Bake in a preheated oven at 180°C for 20–30 minutes until lightly golden. Remove from the oven and let the cake cool completely.
Mix the powdered sugar with enough water to make a thick icing and spread it evenly over the cooled cake.
For the best flavor and texture, refrigerate the cake overnight before serving.

Melt the butter in a heavy saucepan over medium heat. Continue cooking, stirring constantly, for 3–5 minutes until it turns golden brown. Immediately remove from the heat, transfer to a bowl, and refrigerate for 30 minutes until cooled.
Preheat the oven to 175°C.

Beat the cooled browned butter with the granulated sugar until well combined. Add the egg yolk, vanilla extract, and ground cardamom, then mix until smooth. Gradually add the flour on low speed until a soft dough forms.
Shape the dough into 2.5 cm (1-inch) balls and place them about 2.5 cm apart on ungreased baking trays.
Bake for 10–12 minutes until the cookies puff up and are lightly golden with small cracks.
Transfer the cookies from the baking tray and let them cool for 1 minute. Roll them in the powdered sugar while still warm, then roll them again once completely cooled.
Store the cookies between sheets of wax paper in a loosely covered container.
Wash the pumpkin, remove the seeds, and cut it into large cubes. Peel and chop the onion, garlic, carrots, and ginger. Wash and dice the eggplant.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large pot and sauté the onion until translucent. Add the pumpkin, carrots, eggplant, garlic, ginger, and raisins, then cook for several minutes.
Sprinkle in the curry powder and cook briefly until fragrant.
Pour in the chopped tomatoes and coconut milk, season with salt, and simmer for 20 minutes.
Season with salt, black pepper, sugar, and lemon juice to taste. Add the frozen peas and simmer for another 10 minutes.
Serve garnished with roasted cashew nuts and chopped parsley.
Mix flour, water, salt, margarine, and yeast to form a dough. Let it rise for 30 minutes.
Shape the dough into 10 very flat buns and let them rise covered for 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 180°C (fan). Brush buns with milk and sprinkle sesame seeds on top.
Bake the buns for about 12 minutes until lightly golden.
Pan-fry mushrooms briefly and set aside.
Mix ground beef with half an onion and spices, then form very flat patties.
Cook patties and add cheese after flipping.
Assemble mini burgers with mayonnaise, cream cheese, lettuce, tomatoes, olives, mushrooms, and any other desired toppings.
Mix flour, powdered sugar, lemon zest, salt, butter, and a small splash of milk to form a soft dough. Shape it to fit a small round baking form.
Preheat the oven to 160°C (fan).
Peel and slice the apple, then drizzle with lemon juice.
In a pan, heat water and sugar until it foams and begins to caramelize. Add butter and stir quickly, then remove from heat before it burns.
Add the apple slices to the caramel and cook briefly until slightly reduced.
Transfer the caramelized apples into the baking form and press them down evenly.
Flatten the dough and place it over the apples, pressing it gently to cover the surface.
Bake for about 20 minutes until golden and set, then let cool before serving.
Preheat oven to 160°C (fan).
Blend cheese, flour, and all spices together until no large cheese pieces remain.
Add water gradually until a dough forms.
Roll out the dough between baking paper to about half a sheet size, not too thin.
Bake at 160°C fan for about 5 minutes.
Remove from oven, cut into pieces with a pizza cutter, and poke a hole in each piece.
Bake again at 180°C fan until fully crisp, about 10 minutes.
Brown the butter in a pan until golden, making sure not to burn the small solids, then let it cool.
Mix brown sugar and white sugar until fine and well combined.
Add the cooled brown butter and eggs, then mix until smooth.
Add flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt, then mix until a dough forms.
Fold in chocolate and nuts.
Shape into 80 g dough balls and chill in the fridge.
Bake at 190°C for about 15 minutes until golden and slightly underbaked in the center.
Puree the strawberries until smooth and measure out about 500 ml of puree.

Mix a small portion of the strawberry puree with sugar and vanilla pudding powder. Bring the remaining puree to a boil, then remove from heat and stir in the pudding mixture. Boil briefly while stirring until thickened.
Let the strawberry pudding cool completely to room temperature.
Beat the softened butter until creamy, then gradually add the cooled pudding one spoon at a time, mixing well after each addition.
Use the buttercream for filling and decorating cakes.
Preheat oven to 175°C. Prepare a jelly roll pan with parchment paper.
Make the design paste by mixing butter and sugar, then adding flour and egg white until smooth. Divide and color as desired.
Pipe the design onto parchment and freeze to set.
Whisk egg yolks with sugar, then add oil, milk, almond extract, salt, and flour to form batter.
Beat egg whites to medium-stiff peaks and gently fold into batter.
Pour batter over frozen design and bake for about 10 minutes until springy.
Invert cake onto parchment, remove backing, dust with sugar, and roll while warm. Let cool completely.
Prepare filling by whipping cream and mixing with strawberry sugar and gelatin mixture.
Unroll cake, spread filling evenly, then roll tightly and refrigerate until set.
Slice with a serrated knife and serve.
Scoop out 100 ml of lemon juice and mix it with starch. Set aside.
Bring the remaining lemon juice, powdered sugar, and vanilla sugar to a boil, then simmer for 3 minutes.
Add butter or margarine, cream, and lemon zest, then simmer for another 3 minutes.
Stir in the starch mixture and bring to a boil for 1 minute until thickened.
Mix flour, sugar, baking powder, vanilla sugar, salt, bitter almond aroma, butter, and eggs until a sticky dough forms.
Knead in the almonds, nuts, or chocolate chunks until evenly distributed.
Shape the dough into two logs roughly the width of a baking tray.
Bake at 200°C (top/bottom heat) for 15 minutes.
Let cool slightly, then cut diagonally into 1 cm slices.
Bake again for 8 minutes at the same temperature until crisp.
Store airtight to keep the cookies crunchy.
Dissolve yeast in a portion of water, then mix with flour, salt, and remaining water to form a smooth dough. Knead well and let rise for 30–60 minutes.
Shape the dough into rolls of about 75–80 g each and let them rise again for about 30 minutes.
Preheat oven to 220°C (top/bottom heat) or 190°C (fan) and prepare baking tray.
Prepare lye solution carefully by dissolving sodium hydroxide pellets in water. Dip each roll for about 10 seconds per side, then place on baking tray.
Score the rolls with a sharp knife and sprinkle with coarse salt.
Bake for 17–20 minutes until deep brown and crusty.
Optional: For freezing, omit salt topping. Dough can also be cold-fermented overnight with reduced yeast.
Cut chicken wings at the joints and separate them.
Season the wings with salt and let them dry slightly.
Coat the wings with spices and a light layer of flour.
Air fry at 200°C for about 15 minutes until crispy and cooked through.
Do not overcrowd the air fryer basket.
Prepare a preferment (poolish) by mixing part of the water, flour, and yeast and let it ferment. (150 Wasser 2 trockenhefe 150 Mehl)
Prepare a second dough by mixing remaining water, yeast, salt, and flour, then combine with the poolish.
Let the dough rest (autolyse) for 20 minutes.
Knead the dough for about 10 minutes until elastic.
Perform stretch and folds on a floured surface and let rest for 5 minutes.
Repeat folds and rest cycles, allowing long fermentation for 60–120 minutes depending on temperature.
Divide dough into 3 portions and gently shape, folding twice into compact packets.
Let rest for 5 minutes, then gently shape into baguettes without over-flattening.
Final shaping: roll into baguette shape and place on a floured baking tray.
Proof for about 45–60 minutes, then score the surface.
Bake at 250°C (top/bottom heat) with steam for 10 minutes, then 10 minutes without steam, followed by an additional 4 minutes at 200°C if needed.
Cut lemons in half and scoop out the insides to create lemon cups.
Heat cream, sugar, lemon zest, and basil until everything is dissolved and slightly foamy.
Remove basil leaves from the mixture.
Stir in lemon juice thoroughly.
Pour the cream mixture into the hollowed lemon halves.
Chill for about 2 hours before serving.
Cook rice and set aside.
Cook bacon until crispy and set aside.
Season chicken with spices and cook in air fryer at 190°C for about 20 minutes or pan fry until fully cooked.
Blend cottage cheese, mayonnaise, lime juice, and garlic into a creamy sauce.
Cut chicken and mix with rice, bacon, cheese, and sauce.
Fill tortillas with rice mixture and toast in a pan until crisp.

ingredients.name: Tomato paste / chocolate / grated apple / ketchup (umami sweeteners)

instructions.text: Cook rice in a rice cooker with water and set aside.
Chop onions, potatoes, carrots, and meat into bite-sized pieces.
Brown the meat and vegetables in a pan.
Add chicken broth and simmer over medium heat for about 20 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Add curry roux cubes and stir until fully dissolved.
Add optional umami boosters like tomato paste, chocolate, grated apple, or ketchup, then simmer for 5 more minutes.
Chop pumpkin, carrots, onion, and ginger into chunks.
Sauté onion briefly, then add pumpkin, carrots, and ginger.
Add vegetable broth and simmer until vegetables are soft.
Blend until smooth and stir in coconut milk.
Season to taste with balsamic vinegar and/or soy sauce.
Peel, cut, and boil potatoes until soft. Let them steam off to remove excess moisture.
Mash potatoes and mix with egg yolks, Parmesan, salt, garlic, spices, and melted butter.
Add starch if the mixture is too soft, then fold in fresh herbs.
Let the mixture cool slightly, then pipe onto a baking tray.
Bake at 220°C (top/bottom heat) for about 15 minutes or air fry at 200°C for 7 minutes until golden.
Optionally garnish with extra egg yolk, zest, or herbs before serving.
Mix quark, flour, egg, baking powder, and salt into a batter.
Cook the batter in a pan over medium-low heat on both sides until set and golden.
Poach eggs in vinegar water for 3–4 minutes.
Fry the ham in a pan until lightly browned.
Serve the flatbread topped with ham, poached eggs, hollandaise sauce, and fresh vegetables.
Mix flour, salt, yeast, milk, butter, and egg into a dough and let it rise for 1–2 hours until doubled in size.
Prepare garlic butter by mixing softened butter, grated garlic, salt, and parsley.
Divide dough into 4 portions, then further divide each into smaller pieces, flatten into discs, and spread with garlic butter.
Fold, roll, and shape into flower-like buns.
Let rise again for about 30 minutes.
Bake at 180°C (fan) for about 20 minutes until golden and cooked through.
Slice onions and mushrooms, then sauté until reduced in volume by about half.
Remove mushrooms from the pan and melt butter in the same pan.
Add paprika, chili oil, flour, salt, pepper, and dill, and cook into a thick roux.
Deglaze with a splash of white wine and add broth, then return mushrooms to the pot.
Simmer for about 10 minutes until slightly reduced.
Stir in milk and sour cream, quark, or crème fraîche.
Finish with parsley and season carefully with soy sauce, avoiding over-salting.
Mix yeast, sugar, and warm water and let it activate briefly.
Add flour, salt, and oil, then knead into a smooth dough for 5–10 minutes.
Let dough rise for about 1 hour until doubled in size.
Divide dough into 4 portions, flatten into circles, and add filling in the center.
Seal buns and place on parchment squares, then let rest for 15 minutes.
Steam buns over boiling water for about 15 minutes, then rest for 5 minutes without heat.
Slice meat thinly against the grain.
Mix meat with baking powder and enough water to cover, then massage well.
Let rest for about 30 minutes, then rinse and drain thoroughly.
Marinate with soy sauce, garlic, sake, sesame oil, chili, and MSG.
Coat lightly with starch and massage again.
Stir-fry in hot oil until browned and cooked through.
Mix water and dashi powder.
Add flour, egg, salt, and sugar to form a batter and chill in the fridge.
Mix shredded cabbage and chopped spring onion into the batter.
Pour into a pan as a thick pancake and top with bacon pieces.
Cook until crispy, then flip and cook the other side until done.
Top with okonomiyaki sauce, mayonnaise, nori strips, pickled ginger, and bonito flakes.
Optional: serve with quick pickled ginger made from ginger, rice vinegar, and sugar.
Add tofu, banana, milk, cocoa powder, honey, and chia seeds into a blender.
Blend until smooth and creamy.
Serve immediately.
Mix butter, sugar, almond aroma, ground almonds, and baking powder into a dough.
Roll out the dough and shape into small rectangles.
Place whole almonds on top and press lightly into the dough.
Optionally decorate by pressing patterns with a fork from the edges toward the center.
Bake at 180°C (fan) for 12–15 minutes until lightly browned at the edges.

recipes.name: Shallot Tarte Tatin (Upside-Down Puff Pastry Tart)

instructions.text: Peel and halve shallots, then sauté cut-side down until lightly caramelized.
Add water and simmer until shallots are tender, being careful not to burn.
Remove shallots from the pan.
In the same pan, combine balsamic vinegar, brown sugar, mustard, and butter to form a glaze.
Return shallots to the pan and arrange them evenly.
Cover with puff pastry and bake at 180°C (fan) for about 20 minutes until golden.

ingredients.name: Breadcrumb coating (panko / flour + starch / crushed cornflakes)

instructions.text: Cut cauliflower into bite-sized nugget pieces.
Mix egg, quark, flour, oil, salt, and spices into a coating batter.
Coat cauliflower pieces in the batter.
Bread each piece using panko, flour-starch mix, or crushed cornflakes.
Air fry at 180°C for about 10 minutes or bake at 200°C until cooked and crispy.
Serve with a tangy quark dip, optionally flavored with lemon or other acidity.
Slice cucumber and place in a bowl.
Mix fish sauce, sesame oil, soy sauce, rice vinegar, MSG, sugar, chili oil, and garlic oil into a dressing.
Add skyr or quark to the dressing and mix until slightly creamy.
Combine cucumber with dressing and toss well.
Top with chopped spring onion before serving.
Whisk eggs in a bowl until smooth.
Mix in broth powder, salt, warm water, and optional spices.
Remove bubbles from the surface for a smooth texture.
Steam over boiling water for about 13 minutes with lid on.
Top with spring onion, soy sauce, and chili oil before serving.
Finely chop spring onions and blend together with tomatoes, spices, and peanut butter.
Pour mixture into a pan and add chickpeas and white beans.
Add cream and bring to a boil, stirring well.
Simmer until slightly thickened and serve with flatbread.
Cut the chicken thigh into evenly sized pieces.
Marinate the chicken with Shaoxing wine, dark soy sauce, cornflour, baking soda, salt, and pepper.
Cook the chicken over medium to high heat until browned, then set aside.
In an oiled pan, fry garlic, ginger, and the white parts of spring onion, then add chilli.
Add white onion and cook until slightly softened but still crunchy, then return the chicken to the pan.

Pour in the sauce mixture (chicken stock, soy sauce, dark soy sauce, Shaoxing wine, rice wine vinegar, sesame oil, sugar, and cornflour), then add spring onion greens and Sichuan pepper powder. Simmer until thickened.
Finish by stirring in roasted peanuts and serve.

recipes.name: High-Protein Black Bean Quesadilla Meal Prep

instructions.text: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat and cook garlic until fragrant.
Add black beans, taco seasoning, enchilada sauce, nutritional yeast, and chopped cilantro stems. Simmer for about 30 minutes until thickened.
Mash the bean mixture until thick but still textured, then mix in cottage cheese, mozzarella, cheddar, and cilantro leaves. Let cool.
Divide filling across tortillas, fold, and optionally toast until lightly golden. Cool completely and freeze.
Prepare chipotle sauce by blending chipotles, Greek yogurt, honey, avocado oil, and salt.
Season chicken thighs with salt and pepper, then coat lightly in cornstarch or flour.
Pan fry chicken over medium-high heat for 6–8 minutes per side until crispy and cooked through, then set aside.
Melt butter in a pan, then add garlic, soy sauce, honey, lemon zest, paprika, and chili flakes. Stir and reduce until thick and sticky.
Return chicken to the pan and coat thoroughly in the sauce.
Optional: continue reducing remaining sauce until it becomes very thick, then drizzle over the chicken for extra glaze.
Garnish with sesame seeds and green onions and serve.
Marinate chicken with soy sauce, pepper, chili flakes, grated garlic, grated ginger, baking soda, and cornstarch.
Cook chicken in a pan until browned and cooked through, then set aside.
Cook noodles according to package instructions and drain.
In a bowl, mix soy sauce, chili crisp, rice vinegar, sesame oil, brown sugar, peanut butter, and grated garlic to form the sauce.
In a pan, melt butter and toss cooked noodles with sauce until evenly coated.
Add chicken and spring onions, mix well and heat through.
Finish with sesame seeds and serve.
Mix ground beef with grated onion, garlic, chopped red peppers, parsley, chili flakes, paprika, cumin, salt, and black pepper until fully combined.
Spread the meat mixture onto a lightly oiled baking tray and optionally score into portions. Place whole red peppers alongside and drizzle with olive oil.
Bake at 200°C for 30–40 minutes until cooked through and slightly charred, with blistered peppers.
Prepare tahini sauce by mixing tahini, yogurt, lemon juice, garlic, salt, and water until smooth and pourable.
Combine red onion, tomato, parsley, sumac, olive oil, lemon juice, and salt to make onion salad.
Slice baked kebab, warm wraps, and assemble with kebab, roasted peppers, onion salad, and tahini sauce.
Roll wraps tightly and toast briefly in a pan until golden and crisp.
Add rice and water to a rice cooker (using slightly less water than usual).
Add chicken, soy sauce, mirin, garlic, onion powder, and chopped onion directly into the rice cooker.
Cook in the rice cooker until done, allowing flavors to absorb into the rice.
Fluff, mix well, and serve once fully cooked.
Mash kidney beans with canned tomato, cumin, paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper until combined but still chunky.
Mix diced tomato, red onion, and herbs with lime juice and salt to form a fresh salad.
Stir soy yogurt with grated garlic, lime juice, salt, and pepper to make the sauce.
Spread bean mixture onto a wrap, top with salad, romaine lettuce, and yogurt sauce, then roll tightly and serve.

recipes.name: Chinese Braised Chicken Breast with Scallions

instructions.text: Slice chicken breasts into thick pieces and lightly pound to tenderize.
Mix smashed ginger with cooking wine and light soy sauce, then massage into the chicken.
Add egg and cornstarch, mixing until chicken is evenly coated.
Pan-fry chicken until golden on both sides, then remove and set aside.
Fry ginger and scallion whites in the same pan, then layer scallion greens and chicken back in.
Add soy sauces, sugar, five spice, chicken powder, and water. Bring to a boil.
Cover and simmer, then uncover and reduce until sauce becomes glossy.
Finely chop pickles and spring onions.
Mix herb quark, low-fat quark, and cottage cheese until smooth.
Stir in chopped pickles, spring onions, cheddar, Tabasco, and jalapeños.
Optionally fold in cooked bacon, ham, or chicken for extra protein.
Mix well and chill before serving.
Boil eggs until hard boiled, then peel and chop.
Mix tuna, Greek yogurt, and a small amount of wasabi into a creamy dip.
Slice or chop cucumbers for dipping or serving.
Combine chopped eggs with the tuna mixture or serve on top.
Serve chilled with cucumbers on the side.
Whisk soy sauce, hoisin, ketchup, apple juice, water, vinegar, brown sugar, ginger, and garlic into a sauce mixture.
Season chicken thighs with onion powder, garlic powder, paprika, and black pepper.
Brown chicken in a skillet with oil, then remove and set aside.
Pour sauce into the pan and reduce by half, scraping up browned bits, then stir in cornstarch slurry.
Return chicken to the pan and simmer until fully cooked and sauce is thickened.
Marinate chicken with dark soy sauce, light soy sauce, oyster sauce, garlic, brown sugar, honey, rice vinegar, and sesame oil for 15 minutes.
Cook chicken in a pan over medium heat until fully cooked and sauce reduces into a sticky glaze.
Serve over rice once the sauce is thick and glossy.
Optionally garnish with scallions.

recipes.name: Smashed Beef Kabob with Cucumber Yogurt

instructions.text: Mix Greek yogurt with cucumbers, mint, and garlic, then chill until serving.
Combine ground beef with onion, turmeric, 1 teaspoon of salt, and black pepper.
Cook beef in a hot skillet in large chunks until deeply browned and crisp on the bottom, then break apart.
Stir in walnuts and cranberries and cook until toasted and fully browned.
Season yogurt with remaining salt, spread on a plate, top with beef, and finish with pomegranate molasses.
Serve with warm pita or rice.
Sauté shallots and garlic in olive oil until fragrant.
Stir in miso paste and red pepper flakes to build flavor.
Add chickpeas, broth, and cream, then simmer until slightly thickened.
Stir in nutritional yeast, lemon zest, and lemon juice, then adjust seasoning with salt and pepper.
Garnish with fresh parsley and basil before serving.

recipes.name: Crispy Beef Tacos with Honey Chipotle Sauce

instructions.text: Cook onion in avocado oil until soft, then add garlic and ground beef. Brown the beef.
Add taco seasoning spices and tomato paste, then pour in beef stock and simmer until thick and saucy.
Blend all honey chipotle sauce ingredients until smooth.
Mix pico de gallo ingredients (tomato, onion, cilantro, lime, salt, pepper).
Assemble tacos with tortillas, beef, and mozzarella, then fold and fry until crispy.
Serve with pico de gallo and honey chipotle sauce.
Mix gochujang, gochugaru, soy sauce, sweetener, garlic, and water to form sauce.
Microwave cabbage for 2 minutes until softened.
Combine boiled eggs, cabbage, and sauce in a bowl and microwave briefly until heated through.
Top with shredded cheese and green onion before serving.
Cook onions in butter and oil until deeply caramelized.
Add pancetta and cook until crisp, then stir in garlic and white wine.
Season with salt, thyme, and rosemary.
Add pasta and beef broth, then simmer until pasta is cooked and liquid is reduced.
Stir in cream and cheeses until melted and creamy.
Serve hot once sauce is thick and glossy.

recipes.name: High Protein Low Cal Alfredo (Cauliflower Alfredo Sauce)

instructions.text: Cook cauliflower until very soft for a smooth texture.
Blend hot cauliflower with Parmesan, Pecorino Romano, garlic, and skim milk until completely smooth.
Use as a creamy low-calorie Alfredo sauce.
Preheat oven to 415ºF and score potatoes.
Melt butter and mix with parmesan, onion powder, garlic powder, salt, and pepper in a baking dish.
Place potatoes cut-side down and brush with butter mixture, then top with extra parmesan.
Bake until golden and crispy.
Slice leek and spring onions, reserving some green parts for garnish.
Sauté leek and spring onions in olive oil until lightly browned.
Deglaze with miso water, then reduce heat and stir in peanut butter, coconut aminos, sriracha, and grated ginger.
Simmer until creamy and slightly thickened, then add lime juice and chickpeas.
Cook for a few more minutes until heated through.
Top with spring onion greens, sesame, and chili oil before serving.
Blend or mash half of the peas with garlic, nutritional yeast, and water until slightly smooth.
Mix in remaining peas, chopped herbs, flour, salt, and pepper until combined.
Shape mixture into small patties and cook in a pan or oven until crispy.
Mix yogurt, lemon juice, herbs, and salt to make the dip.
Serve fritters warm with the yogurt dip.
Boil eggs for 12 minutes, then cool in ice water.
Peel eggs, separate yolks from whites, and add yolks to a blender.
Blend egg yolks with olive oil, Worcestershire sauce, vinegar or pickle juice, salt, and pepper until smooth.
Use as a high-protein low-calorie mayo alternative.
Gently cook garlic in butter until caramelized, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Let the melted butter cool until no longer hot.
Blend butter with caramelized garlic until smooth and creamy.
Chill for 30 minutes in the refrigerator.
Serve on fresh bread with sea salt.

recipes.name: Garlic Butter Chicken with Crispy Soy Honey Glaze

instructions.text: Dredge chicken in flour, egg, and crushed cornflakes, then air fry at 385°F for 16 minutes, flipping halfway until cooked through.
Make garlic butter soy glaze by cooking butter, garlic, soy sauce, brown sugar, honey, and water until thickened.
Mix sour cream with sriracha for a spicy sauce.
Prepare cucumber salad with cucumber, red onion, avocado oil, hot sauce, garlic, parsley, and red pepper flakes.
Assemble sandwich using ciabatta, crispy chicken, garlic butter soy glaze, sour cream sauce, and cucumber salad.
Press boiled potatoes into a greased baking dish and pre-bake at 200°C until slightly crisp.
Add chopped leek and lightly cooked sausage on top.
Mix cream cheese, cream, eggs, salt, and pepper into a smooth sauce.
Pour sauce over the layers and top with shredded cheese.
Bake at 160°C until golden and set, then serve hot.
Sear ground beef in oil until browned, then remove from pan.
Sauté onion, carrots, and celery until browned, then add garlic.
Stir in tomato paste and flour, cooking briefly.
Deglaze with red wine, then add beef stock, thyme, and beef. Simmer until thickened.
Prepare mashed potatoes by mixing cooked potatoes with butter and egg yolk.
Assemble beef filling in a baking dish, top with mashed potatoes, and bake until golden.
Mix egg, flour, spices, and seasoning to create a coating mixture.
Coat cauliflower florets in the mixture, then cover with panko breadcrumbs.
Air fry at 180°C for 10 minutes until crispy.
Mix cream cheese and milk to make a herb dip.
Serve cauliflower nuggets with dip.

recipes.name: Potato Mochi with Sweet Soy Sauce Glaze

instructions.text: Boil and mash potato until smooth.
Mix mashed potato with starch and salt to form a dough.
Shape into small pieces and pan-fry until golden and crispy.
Make glaze by combining mirin, soy sauce, brown sugar, starch, and water, then simmer until thickened.
Coat potato mochi in sweet soy glaze and serve.
Blanch napa cabbage leaves in boiling water until pliable, then transfer to cold water.
Chop carrot, green onions, and shrimp into small pieces.
Mix shrimp, vegetables, garlic, sesame oil, soy sauce, and black pepper in a bowl.
Fill cabbage leaves with mixture and roll into dumplings.
Pan-sear dumplings until browned and shrimp is fully cooked.
Serve with ponzu sauce drizzled on top.

recipes.name: Crispy Rice Paper Gimbap Roll with Gochujang Tofu

instructions.text: Mix tofu with gochujang, coconut aminos, rice vinegar, maple syrup, and water to make a glaze.
Prepare fillings by slicing cucumber, avocado, and shredding cabbage.
Assemble rice paper rolls with rice, tofu, vegetables, and seaweed, then sprinkle with sesame seeds.
Pan-fry rolls in avocado oil until crispy on all sides.
Serve hot and crispy.

ingredients.name: Döner seasoning or seasoning of choice

instructions.text: Mix the spelt flour, protein cream powder, low-fat quark, baking powder, salt, and water into a smooth dough. Shape into two flatbreads.
Bake the flatbreads at 180°C fan oven without preheating for 12–15 minutes. Do not let them brown too much or they will become dry.
Meanwhile, fry the chicken breast slices in a pan using oil spray and season with döner seasoning or your preferred spices.
Prepare the sauce by mixing the yogurt with the optional garlic, salt, black pepper, fresh parsley, and fresh chives.
Slice the lettuce, onion, and tomatoes.
Cut open the baked flatbreads, fill them with the chicken, lettuce, onion, tomatoes, and sauce. Sprinkle with sesame seeds before serving.

Mix the flour, cold water, and yeast in a bowl and begin kneading. Once the flour has absorbed the water, add the olive oil and sea salt. Knead until smooth, then transfer the dough to the work surface, stretch and fold it a few times, and shape it into a tight ball.
Place the dough in a lightly greased bowl, cover it, and let it ferment at room temperature for 12–24 hours.

The next day, slice the red onions, season them with a little salt, and set aside. Dust your work surface with flour, gently turn out the dough, dust the top with more flour, and divide it into four equal portions.

Use your fingertips to press each dough portion into a flatbread with small dimples. Spread each with crème fraîche seasoned with salt and black pepper. Squeeze excess moisture from the onions, then top with the onions and bacon cubes.
Transfer the flatbreads to a pizza peel or cutting board and bake in a preheated 250°C oven on a baking tray, pizza stone, or baking steel for 8–10 minutes.
Sprinkle with fresh chives after baking and serve immediately.

recipes.name: Duck Pan Sauce with Orange and Grand Marnier

instructions.text: Score the skin of the duck breasts and sear them in a pan until the skin is crispy and caramelized. Remove the duck and keep the browned bits in the pan for the sauce.
Add the diced shallot to the pan and cook until softened.
Deglaze the pan with Grand Marnier, then add orange marmalade, orange juice, chicken or veal stock, soy sauce, and thyme.
Reduce the sauce until it thickens and the flavors concentrate. Remove from heat and whisk in cold butter for a glossy finish.
Serve the sauce over the duck breasts, mashed potatoes, rice, or another side dish.

ingredients.name: White fish (such as cod or snapper)

instructions.text: Boil the russet potatoes until tender. Mash them with salted butter, egg yolks, salt, and white pepper until smooth and creamy.
Prepare the filling vegetables by slicing the leeks, shallot, fennel, and brown mushrooms.
Cook the vegetables in butter until softened. Add flour and stir to create a roux.
Gradually add whole milk and heavy cream while stirring until the sauce thickens.
Add the salmon, white fish, seaweed seasoning, dill, parsley, salt, and pepper. Cook gently until the fish is just cooked through.
Transfer the fish filling into a baking dish and spread the mashed potatoes evenly over the top.
Bake until the potato topping is golden and the filling is bubbling. Serve warm.
Slice off the root ends and caps of the king oyster mushrooms. Cut the remaining stems into 2.5 cm thick rounds. Score one or both sides of the mushroom rounds in a criss cross pattern.

Place the mushroom pieces flat in a pan. Add vegetable stock and dried kombu, cover with a lid, and bring to a boil. Cook for a few minutes until the mushrooms release liquid, then remove the lid and boil until all liquid has evaporated.
Heat olive oil in a clean pan and sear the mushroom rounds until golden brown on both sides.
Add vegan butter and let it melt. Add minced garlic and cook for about 2 minutes. Add lemon juice, salt, and black pepper, then swirl everything together for another 20 seconds.
Add fresh parsley and toss the mushrooms until coated. Arrange the mushroom scallops on a plate, spoon over the remaining lemon butter sauce, and serve.

recipes.name: Jalapeño Salmon Bites in Crispy Rice Paper

instructions.text: Cut the salmon into 2.5 cm cubes. Season with salt and smoked paprika and set aside.
Mix garlic, ginger, soy sauce, brown sugar, red pepper flakes, and sriracha sauce together to make the marinade.
Add the salmon to the marinade and coat well. Reserve one quarter of the sauce as a dipping sauce. Marinate the salmon for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight.
Fry the salmon bites in a pan over medium heat until golden brown and cooked through, about 3–4 minutes per side. Remove from heat.

Dip the rice paper sheets in warm water. Add two salmon cubes, one slice of jalapeño, and one small avocado wedge. Fold the sides and roll into a small package. Wrap each bite again with another rice paper sheet for a double layer.

Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Fry the wrapped bites in a single layer without overcrowding until crispy and golden brown, about 3–4 minutes per side. Serve with the reserved dipping sauce.

Prepare two shallow dishes. Mix flour, salt, black pepper, and garlic powder in one dish. Whisk eggs and parmesan together in the second dish. Slice each chicken breast horizontally through the centre to create four even pieces, then coat each piece in the flour mixture.

Heat olive oil and 15g butter in a large non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Dip each chicken piece into the egg mixture, let excess drip off, and cook for about 3 minutes per side until golden and nearly cooked through. Remove the chicken. Fry the lemon slices for about 20 seconds per side until slightly softened and coloured, then set aside.

Pour the white wine into the pan and simmer rapidly for about 2 minutes until mostly evaporated, scraping up the browned bits from the pan. Add chicken stock and 1 tablespoon lemon juice, then bring to a simmer.

Coat the cold butter cubes with leftover flour, add them to the pan, and whisk quickly until incorporated into the sauce. Add chives and continue simmering while stirring until the sauce becomes glossy and thick. Season to taste.
Return the chicken and any resting juices to the pan. Place the lemon slices on top, baste with the sauce to warm through, and serve.
Use a melon baller to create melon balls, or cut the melon into 1/2–1 inch cubes.

Heat a skillet over medium heat. Place prosciutto slices flat in the pan and cook for 1–2 minutes until they begin to crisp and shrink. Flip and cook for another minute until crispy. Repeat with remaining slices, then cool and roughly chop.
Arrange the salad on a platter with a base of arugula, then add melon, burrata, crispy prosciutto, and chopped basil.
Drizzle with olive oil and balsamic reduction. Season with flaky salt and black pepper to taste. Avoid adding too much salt because the prosciutto is already salty.
Add spinach, cherry tomatoes, the exposed garlic bulb, and vegan cream cheese to a baking dish. Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt and black pepper.
Bake at 180°C for 30–40 minutes until the tomatoes are soft and the garlic is roasted.
Cook the pasta according to the package instructions.
Squeeze the roasted garlic out of its skin and mix it with the baked ingredients to create a creamy sauce.
Add the cooked pasta to the baking dish, mix everything together, and serve.

recipes.name: Steak & Stout Pie with Mash and Gravy

instructions.text: Heat oil in a large ovenproof casserole dish. Season and brown the beef in batches, then set aside.
Reduce the heat and cook onions, celery, and garlic until softened, about 8–10 minutes. Return the beef to the pan and add thyme, bay leaves, and beef stock.
Add stout and carrots. Bring to a boil, then cook covered in the oven at 150°C for 2–2.5 hours until the beef is tender. Adjust seasoning and allow the filling to cool.
Line a pie dish with shortcrust pastry. Blind bake with baking beans for 15 minutes at 200°C, remove the beans, and bake for a few more minutes. Let cool and tidy the edges.
Peel and chop the potatoes into medium chunks. Boil in salted water until fork-tender, then mash or pass through a potato ricer. Melt butter with milk and mix into the mash. Season to taste.

Fill the pastry-lined dish with the steak filling. Brush the pastry edge with beaten egg, place the pastry lid on top, seal, trim, and crimp the edges. Create steam vents and decorate with leftover pastry if desired. Brush the top with remaining egg wash.
Bake the pie at 200°C for 30–35 minutes until golden and crisp.
For the gravy, melt butter in a pan, add flour, and cook for 2 minutes while stirring. Add red wine, then whisk in beef stock. Simmer until thickened and season to taste.
Serve the pie on top of the mashed potatoes and pour over the gravy.
Wash the rice and add it to the rice cooker with the usual amount of water used for cooking rice.
Mix soy sauce, hondashi, mirin, and sugar together in a bowl to create the seasoning sauce.
Add diced chicken thighs and thinly sliced onion to the sauce and toss until coated.
Place the chicken and onion mixture directly over the rice in the rice cooker. Pour over any remaining sauce from the bowl.
Cook the rice normally. While it cooks, crack the eggs into the same bowl and beat them lightly with chopsticks or a fork for about 20 seconds.
Immediately after the rice finishes cooking, quickly open the rice cooker, pour the beaten eggs over the chicken and rice, close it again, and cook for another 2 minutes on the regular rice setting.
Open the rice cooker and serve garnished with green onions and roasted sesame seeds.
Cut the cauliflower into medium-sized florets.
Prepare two bowls: one with flour, plant milk, garlic powder, smoked paprika, nutritional yeast, salt, and pepper to make the batter, and one with breadcrumbs.
Coat the cauliflower florets in the batter, then cover them with breadcrumbs and place them on a baking tray. Drizzle with oil.
Bake at 200°C for 20–30 minutes until crispy and golden.

For the sticky sauce, sauté scallions, garlic, and ginger in sesame oil for a few minutes. Add sesame seeds and toast briefly, then add soy sauce, brown sugar, rice vinegar, and cornstarch dissolved in water. Cook until the sauce thickens.
Add the crispy cauliflower to the sauce and stir until evenly coated.
Serve topped with more sesame seeds and the green parts of the scallions. Enjoy with rice or as desired.
Cut the bread rolls or pretzels into small cubes.
Finely dice the onions and sauté them in butter until translucent. Add bacon cubes if desired and cook briefly.
Add the bread cubes, eggs, milk, chopped parsley, salt, pepper, nutmeg, and the onion mixture to a bowl.
Knead everything together well, season generously to taste, and let the mixture rest for about 10 minutes.
Transfer the mixture into a baking dish and bake at 200°C top and bottom heat for about 30 minutes until golden brown.
Mash or crumble the tofu and mix it with chickpea flour, salt, curry powder, and paprika until a dough forms.
Shape the tofu mixture into small balls.
Mix the tofu balls with cornstarch and oil until coated.
Bake the tofu puffs at 180°C for 20 minutes until lightly crispy.
For the sweet-spicy glaze, combine gochujang, vinegar, agave syrup, and ketchup. Heat until the sauce thickens.
Add the baked tofu puffs to the glaze and stir until evenly coated. Serve warm.
Cut the tomatoes and red onion into large pieces. Add them to a baking tray with garlic, coat with olive oil, and season with salt, pepper, cayenne pepper, and nutmeg.
Roast the vegetables until softened and caramelized.
Transfer the roasted vegetables to a pot. Add balsamic vinegar and vegetable stock, then blend until smooth.
Stir in heavy cream and adjust seasoning to taste.
Serve warm and garnish with fresh basil.

recipes.name: BBQ Salmon with Mango Avocado Salsa

instructions.text: Season the salmon with garlic powder, onion powder, smoked paprika, chili powder, and brown sugar.
Cook the seasoned salmon using your preferred BBQ method until juicy and cooked through.
Dice the mango, avocado, and red onion. Mix together with lime juice and zest, honey, and salt to create the salsa.
Serve the BBQ salmon with the mango avocado salsa and rice.
Heat 1.5 teaspoons of sesame oil in a non-stick wok or pan over medium-high heat.
Crumble the tofu into a bowl until it resembles a mince-like texture, then mix with cornstarch.
Add the tofu to the wok and stir in 0.5 tablespoon of dark soy sauce. Cook for about 10 minutes until crispy, stirring occasionally to break up the tofu.
Mix the remaining dark soy sauce with tahini, ginger, Chinese five spice powder, chilli oil, and 2 tablespoons of water. Stir well and set aside.
Cook the noodles for 2–4 minutes until al dente.
Transfer the crispy tofu to a bowl. Wipe the wok clean, return it to the heat, add the remaining sesame oil, and fry the pak choi for about 1 minute.
Reduce the heat and add the cooked noodles, crispy tofu, prepared sauce, and most of the spring onions. Stir and heat through for 30 seconds, avoiding overcooking the noodles.
Serve in bowls topped with the remaining spring onions and extra chilli oil if desired.
Finely chop the tomatoes, red onion, parsley, spring onion, mint, and dill.
Mix the chopped vegetables and herbs with vegan feta cheese, flour, salt, and pepper until a thick mixture forms.
Shape the mixture into small patties or fritters.
Heat olive oil in a pan and fry the tomato fritters until golden and crispy on both sides.
Serve warm as an appetizer or snack.

recipes.name: Creamy Sweet Potato and Chickpea Curry

instructions.text: Peel and dice the sweet potato. Place it in a saucepan with enough water to cover, bring to a boil, then reduce heat and cook for about 15 minutes until tender. Drain.
Blend the cooked sweet potato with coconut milk, vegetable broth, curry powder, ginger, smoked paprika, cayenne pepper, salt, and pepper until smooth. Set aside.
Finely dice the onion and garlic. Drain the chickpeas. Heat olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat, then sauté the onion for 2–3 minutes. Add garlic and chickpeas and cook for another 1–2 minutes.
Pour in the sweet potato mixture and stir. Reduce heat to low-medium and cook for 10–15 minutes, stirring regularly, until the curry thickens.
Serve hot with rice and optionally garnish with coconut cream and fresh herbs.

recipes.name: Pesto Pasta with Peas, Lemon, Chilli and Garlic

instructions.text: Cook the pasta in a large pot of very salty water. Reserve some pasta water before draining.
Dice the chilli and garlic. Sauté them in olive oil for a couple of minutes until fragrant.
Add the peas and cook until heated through.
Add the drained pasta to the pan. Stir in pesto and enough reserved pasta water to create a smooth, saucy consistency.
Finish with a generous squeeze of lemon juice and serve.
Cook the pasta according to the package instructions until al dente. Reserve some pasta water before draining.
Blend the roasted peppers, nutritional yeast, garlic, salt, and pepper until smooth.
Add the blended pepper sauce to a pan and warm it through.
Drain the chickpeas and stir them into the sauce.
Add the cooked pasta and some reserved pasta water. Stir until the sauce becomes smooth and silky.
Serve with fresh basil if desired.
